schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: Company Secretary Services — ROC

mainEntity.name: Is a full-time Company Secretary mandatory?

acceptedAnswer.text: Under Section 203, companies with paid-up capital ≥₹10 crore must appoint a full-time CS. Below this, a practicing CS handles compliance on a retainer basis.

mainEntity.name: What happens if annual filings are late?

acceptedAnswer.text: Additional fee of ₹100/day per form (no maximum cap). Persistent default can lead to "Active non-compliant" status and director disqualification under Section 164(2).

mainEntity.name: Do you handle company winding up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Voluntary winding up (Section 59 of IBC) and strike off (Section 248 of Companies Act) for defunct companies. Timeline: 3-6 months.

mainEntity.name: Can you draft board resolutions for ESOP/FEMA transactions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We draft resolutions for ESOP schemes (Section 62(1)(b)), preferential allotment to foreign investors (Section 62(1)(c)), share transfers, capital increases, and all corporate actions — coordinated with FEMA and tax compliance requirements.

mainEntity.name: What does a company secretary do?

acceptedAnswer.text: Statutory compliance management (ROC filings, board meetings, AGM), corporate governance advisory, share transfer/allotment processing, regulatory filings (FEMA, SEBI if applicable), maintenance of statutory registers, and minutes drafting.

mainEntity.name: Is CS mandatory for all companies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mandatory for: companies with paid-up capital ≥₹5 crore (Section 203). Not mandatory for smaller companies but highly recommended. Listed companies: mandatory under SEBI LODR. LLPs: no CS requirement.

mainEntity.name: What statutory registers must a company maintain?

acceptedAnswer.text: Register of Members (MGT-1), Register of Directors/KMP (DIR-12), Register of Charges, Register of Contracts (Section 189), Minutes Book for board and general meetings, Register of Loans/Investments/Guarantees (Section 186).

mainEntity.name: How many board meetings are required?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minimum 4 per calendar year. Maximum gap between 2 meetings: 120 days. First meeting within 30 days of incorporation. Quorum: 1/3 of total directors or 2, whichever is higher. Video conference permitted with conditions.

mainEntity.name: What is the cost of CS retainer?

acceptedAnswer.text: From ₹10,000/month for standard compliance (board meetings, statutory registers, ROC filings). Full secretarial audit: ₹50,000-₹1,00,000 annually. One-time assignments (share transfer, director change): ₹5,000-₹15,000 per transaction.
